items.q: Does a rise in the minimum wage only affect businesses badly?
items.correct: It raises labour costs, but it can also boost worker motivation and increase consumer spending power, and it applies equally to competitors.
items.misconception: A higher minimum wage can only harm businesses.
items.random_wrong: The minimum wage is set separately by each business.
items.why: One-sided answers about legislation lose evaluation marks at GCSE.

items.q: Are exchange rates only relevant to giant multinationals?
items.correct: No — small firms importing materials, or selling to tourists and online customers abroad, are affected by currency movements too.
items.misconception: Exchange rates don't matter unless a firm owns overseas factories.
items.random_wrong: Exchange rates are the prices charged for exchanging faulty goods.
items.why: Students underestimate indirect currency exposure through supply chains and customers.

items.q: Is new technology always an opportunity for existing businesses?
items.correct: No — it can cut costs and open markets, but it can also make products obsolete, enable new competitors and require costly investment to keep up.
items.misconception: Technological change benefits every established firm.
items.random_wrong: Businesses can legally opt out of technological change.
items.why: Technology as both threat and opportunity is the classic external influences discussion.

items.q: An ageing population is which PESTLE factor?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Social. “Political” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: A government raising the minimum wage is which factor?
items.why: Correct answer: Legal. “Technological” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Sales fall from £200,000 to £170,000 during a recession. What is the % fall, in %?
items.why: Exact answer: 15. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
